metadata.description: Production-grade 24-hour time-varying traffic model for Maharashtra 108/112 EMS simulation — all 36 districts, 6 administrative divisions
metadata.data_source: Calibrated from Maharashtra Traffic Police Annual Report 2024 + EMRI 108 Dispatch Logs + NHAI Traffic Census + IMD Weather Data
metadata.calibration_note: Travel times are operational EMS times including dispatch + en-route + scene access. Actual ambulance odometer distances are 8-12% longer than crow-fly due to road network curvature.
metadata.reward_integration_note: Traffic state updates every 3 simulation steps. Agent observation includes current OD matrix slice, active slowdowns, diversion flags, and demand forecast.

global_hour_multipliers.note: Applied on top of zone-specific profiles. Peak morning 7-9h, peak evening 17-19h.

market_day_rural.note: Applied to rural zones on Thursday/Saturday

summer.label: March–May
summer.notes: Reduced traffic overall but heat events increase EMS demand. Road tar softening slows heavy vehicles.

Z27.reason: Nagpur extreme heat, reduced outdoor activity concentrates in AC zones

monsoon.label: June–September
monsoon.notes: Most critical season. Mumbai–Pune axis and all coastal zones severely affected. Ghat sections frequently closed.

Z03.reason: Thane creek and Ulhas river zones

post_monsoon.label: October–November
post_monsoon.notes: Roads under repair, potholes. Festival season increases traffic density.

winter.label: December–February
winter.notes: Generally good roads. Fog affects Vidarbha and Marathwada at night/early morning.

zone_traffic_profiles.note: Per-zone traffic characteristics used to weight global multipliers. Each zone has a base_congestion_level, road_quality, ems_priority_effectiveness, and hourly_traffic_index.

ambulance_staging_posts.note: 72 staging posts across 36 zones. Each post has zone, coordinates, unit types, and shift coverage.

bottleneck_network.note: 54 bottleneck nodes. Each has zone, affected zones, incident slowdown multiplier, clearance time, and EMS bypass route.

BN_11.name: Khopoli Ghat Entry
BN_11.notes: No bypass. Major incident causes 75+ min delay.

BN_12.name: Alibag Ferry Point
BN_12.notes: Ferry crossing — critical delay if missed

ems_emergency_corridors.note: 18 named EMS corridors used for green corridor activation and priority routing. Each corridor connects major zone pairs via optimal hospital routes.

EC_04.name: Mumbai-Pune Expressway MCI Route
EC_04.notes: Fastest Mumbai-Pune route for critical trauma

EC_08.name: Gadchiroli Air Evacuation Corridor
EC_08.notes: Ground route 220+ min. Air preferred for P1.

EC_10.name: Vidarbha Snakebite Corridor
EC_10.notes: Critical for Vidarbha snakebite belt. ASV availability at destination critical.

EC_15.name: Raigad Industrial Emergency Corridor
EC_15.notes: Rasayani and ONGC industrial zone coverage

EC_16.name: Nagpur Vidarbha Air Bridge
EC_16.route: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar International Airport Nagpur
EC_16.notes: NDRF air transport activation threshold: 3+ critical patients

EC_17.name: Kolhapur-Sangli Flood MCI Corridor
EC_17.notes: Flood season activates alternate boat corridors

EC_18.name: Pune-Nashik Expressway Trauma Route
EC_18.notes: High accident zone — steep ghat section

ghat_sections.name: Tamhini Ghat
ghat_sections.notes: Not recommended for ambulances — steep, narrow

bridge_crossings.note: Critical single-bridge points where incident blocks entire route

zone_hourly_incident_probability.note: Probability per step of a traffic incident occurring in each zone, by hour. Used by incident_engine to spawn secondary slowdowns.

zone_hourly_ems_demand_index.note: Relative incident rate by zone and hour. 1.0 = average. Used by demand_forecaster.py to build 12-hour heatmap.

festival_event_calendar.note: Annual recurring events with traffic impact. Month 0 = dynamic (declared). Entries used to adjust OD matrix and activate EMS pre-positioning.

weather_impact_model.note: Weather events and their EMS traffic impact. Triggered by weather station readings.

zone_pair_travel_modifiers.note: Route-specific multipliers applied on top of base OD matrix. Captures real-world asymmetries (one-way systems, toll plazas, railway crossings).

historical_ems_performance.note: Historical response time benchmarks per zone, calibrated from EMRI 108 dispatch data. Used to score agent against real-world baseline.

base_od_matrix_minutes.description: Base travel time matrix in minutes between all 36 zones under free-flow conditions. EMS priority travel = base × 0.65 (urban) or 0.80 (rural). Update every 3 simulation steps using hour_multiplier × season_multiplier × day_multiplier × terrain_factor × zone_profile_factor.

simulation_seeded_scenarios.note: 9 seeded scenarios, one per task. Same seed always produces same traffic state for grader determinism.
